content:
First, note  
  S = 9 + 9² + ⋯ + 9²⁰¹⁰   
is a geometric sum:  
  S = (9³⁰¹¹ – 9)∕(9 – 1) = (9²⁰¹¹ – 9)/8.  

We check primes p in increasing order:

1) p = 2. 9 ≡ 1 (mod 2), so each 9ᵏ ≡ 1, and S ≡ 2010·1 ≡ 0 (mod 2).  ⇒ 2 ∣ S.  
2) p = 3. 9 ≡ 0 (mod 3) ⇒ every term 9ᵏ ≡ 0 ⇒ S ≡ 0 (mod 3). ⇒ 3 ∣ S.  
3) p = 5. 9 ≡ 4 (mod 5), 4² ≡ 1 ⇒ period 2 ⇒ S ≡ (4+1)·1005 ≡ 0 (mod 5). ⇒ 5 ∣ S.  
4) p = 7. 9 ≡ 2 (mod 7), 2³ ≡ 1 ⇒ period 3 ⇒ S ≡ (2+4+1)·670 ≡ 0 (mod 7). ⇒ 7 ∣ S.  
5) p = 11. 9⁵ ≡ 1 (mod 11)), sum of one period = 22 ⇒ 11 ∣ S.  
6) p = 13. 9³ ≡ 1 (mod 13)), sum of one period = 13 ⇒ 13 ∣ S.  

Next prime is 17.  One finds ord₁₇(9)=8, the sum over each cycle of length 8 is 68 ≡ 0 (mod 17), but  
  2010 ≡ 2 (mod 8),  
so the incomplete last cycle contributes 9 + 13 ≡ 22 ≡ 5 (mod 17).  
Hence S ≡ 5 ≠ 0 (mod 17), and 17 ∤ S.  

Therefore the smallest prime not dividing S is  
\boxed{17}.

First, note  
  S = 9 + 9² + ⋯ + 9²⁰¹⁰   
is a geometric sum:  
  S = (9³⁰¹¹ – 9)∕(9 – 1) = (9²⁰¹¹ – 9)/8.  

We check primes p in increasing order:

1) p = 2. 9 ≡ 1 (mod 2), so each 9ᵏ ≡ 1, and S ≡ 2010·1 ≡ 0 (mod 2).  ⇒ 2 ∣ S.  
2) p = 3. 9 ≡ 0 (mod 3) ⇒ every term 9ᵏ ≡ 0 ⇒ S ≡ 0 (mod 3). ⇒ 3 ∣ S.  
3) p = 5. 9 ≡ 4 (mod 5), 4² ≡ 1 ⇒ period 2 ⇒ S ≡ (4+1)·1005 ≡ 0 (mod 5). ⇒ 5 ∣ S.  
4) p = 7. 9 ≡ 2 (mod 7), 2³ ≡ 1 ⇒ period 3 ⇒ S ≡ (2+4+1)·670 ≡ 0 (mod 7). ⇒ 7 ∣ S.  
5) p = 11. 9⁵ ≡ 1 (mod 11)), sum of one period = 22 ⇒ 11 ∣ S.  
6) p = 13. 9³ ≡ 1 (mod 13)), sum of one period = 13 ⇒ 13 ∣ S.  

Next prime is 17.  One finds ord₁₇(9)=8, the sum over each cycle of length 8 is 68 ≡ 0 (mod 17), but  
  2010 ≡ 2 (mod 8),  
so the incomplete last cycle contributes 9 + 13 ≡ 22 ≡ 5 (mod 17).  
Hence S ≡ 5 ≠ 0 (mod 17), and 17 ∤ S.  

Therefore the smallest prime not dividing S is  
\boxed{17}.
